column48:
I’m drawn to underwater filming not only to capture the beauty of the ocean but also to tell human stories within it. My dream is to master the craft, learning how to work with light, movement, and gear underwater. I would like to create films that balance documentary realism with cinematic artistry, while also advocating for conservation and connection to the sea.

Inspired by my girlfriend Ella, I especially want to film her grace and art as a free divers. To me, these movements are like poetry, a form of meditation and dance beneath the surface. My aspiration is to capture that elegance on film, collaborating to create evocative pieces that show diving as both an art form and a deep expression of our relationship with the ocean.
column49:
I’ve always been fascinated by the ocean, it feels like stepping into another world full of mystery, silence, and beauty. What drew me to underwater filming is the chance to capture that world and share it with others, not just through images of marine life, but by telling deeper stories of our connection to the sea. I’m especially inspired by the stories that are told filming the elegance and art of diving, turning it into visual poetry.

I chose BTM Academy because I want to learn from people who truly understand both the technical craft and the artistic side of underwater storytelling. The structured training, expert guidance, and community of like-minded creatives make it the perfect place to develop the skills I need not just to document the ocean, but to bring out its emotion, artistry, and human connection.

I like to go critter hunt in every dive, and focus on marine life shots, rare, hard to find creatures, kinda like a nat geo style shooting, but want to learn how to improve my story telling and camera skills. 
I’ve done a few videos at my fav dive sites in Philippines and Vancouver island, from macro to big animals, pigmy seahorses and nudibranchs to packs of sea lions and orcas, They were personal vacation videos of footage I’ve collected while working at a dive shop.  I like adding music and amplify the experience of the audience, sharing to my friends and family. Other videos more about showing as many highlights of animals filmed at the time. I’ve focused lately on photography but I think I’m more a video guy. Behind the mask videos are simply the best I’ve seen and I aspire to tell stories that good! 
I want to learn as much as I can and keep growing and improving.

column48: Photography entered my life from a very early age, I think I was about 8 years old, a strong influence from my father. I have always been known among my friends as the paparazzi. :) In 2006 I took a diving course, a year after entering the University of Algarve in marine biology, and my vision of the underwater world and wildlife took on other shapes and colors, those of the seabed. With the Adavance Open Water Diver I immediately and without thinking, took up photography and video without mastering any underwater technique. As soon as I had the opportunity, I started investing money in cameras. I have been taking amateur underwater photographs since 2007/2008. But my biggest dream is to be part of something like the Behind the Mask family and make a living from photography and video.
column49: I'm a huge fan of your content and I've been following you since 2015 when one day I saw a video of yours on Facebook or Instagram and then I subscribed to your YouTube channel and since then I've been a huge follower. I love your videos and my biggest dream would be to be part of Behind the Mask, as a marine biologist and experienced diver (with more than 2000 dives) in several different countries and environments. I love any form of wildlife and I am fascinated by capturing every detail and sharing it with the world, with those who are interested and who find this type of content meaningful. column48:
All my life, I have dreamed of traveling and documenting the depths of the ocean. Like many, I have learned on my own and have faced a profession that, in my country, lacks schools, academies, and specialized courses, in addition to having limited resources. Despite this, I have managed to build a career and make a living from it.

However, I am still not where I want to be. Although I have had the opportunity to travel and work with various organizations, I have not yet made the leap to the big leagues or produced full documentaries at the level I aspire to. I feel that my passion is limited by the experience I have been able to build on my own and that I need support to unlock my full potential. I have invested in good equipment and dedicate every day to this profession, but I still haven't been able to make the most of it.
column49:
Nowadays, social media exposes us to the work of many people, and in the end, content gets lost among countless others. However, when I see BTM’s content, it stays in my mind; it stands out from the rest and doesn’t fade away. It remains as a true showcase of work and talent.

That is what I aspire to achieve: to create stories that are free, authentic, and leave a lasting impact. I want to develop the ability to convey solid ideas with rhythm and a unique style, while always surprising, one after another. I am confident that this academy will help me achieve my goals.
column51: I would like to know more about the curriculum and what opportunities are available after completing your course. I’m also interested in whether there are any plans to collaborate with students in the future.
column52: If you are looking for someone who is hardworking, relentless, has a growth mindset, and has proven their passion and determination up to this day, believe me, I am here—ready to learn from you and also to contribute.

column48:
My ultimate vision for our underwater filming journey, with our company ‘Submerge’, is to elevate underwater visuals from a niche specialty to a sought-after, impactful storytelling medium within the commercial landscape. My name is Justin Lutsky, and alongside my creative partner and wife, Abbey Blake, we've built a strong foundation in crafting high-end underwater imagery, combining my expertise in underwater photography and filmmaking with her unique talent as an underwater model.

While we've achieved success with personal clients, our aspiration is to break into larger-scale, high-budget commercial and film projects. We dream of collaborating with brands, businesses and creative storytellers that recognize the power of compelling underwater narratives, producing visuals that not only showcase our technical proficiency but also resonate deeply with a wider audience. We envision Submerge becoming synonymous with breathtaking, impactful underwater storytelling, driving brand engagement and inspiring viewers on a global scale.

My ultimate goal is to see our work utilized in major advertising campaigns, film productions, impactful documentaries, and innovative media partnerships, where our visuals can truly shine and make a significant impact. We want to move beyond simply capturing beautiful images to creating narratives that captivate, educate, and inspire action, whether it’s for environmental awareness, product promotion, or artistic expression.
column49: We’re driven by a passion to share the magic of the underwater world with a broader audience, and we believe our unique skillset and artistic vision can significantly contribute to the commercial space. We see this mentorship as a crucial step in bridging the gap between our creative passion and commercial success. We want to learn how to strategically position our work, attract high-caliber clients, and build a sustainable business model that allows us to pursue our vision of impactful underwater storytelling on a grander scale.

column49:
My passion for filming started when I was a child. Later on, I studied cinema at ESCAC in Barcelona. After that, I began to travel and discovered scuba diving and the beauty of the underwater world. For some time now, I’ve been trying to merge both passions into one.

At one point, a friend and I tried to create our own label. But he moved 700 km away and started a family with two children. I had to take a job as a sales executive to pay the bills, and the project was left halfway—despite some great work done, valuable equipment invested, and a strong desire to keep going- 

While preparing our first project, we discovered your label BTM thanks to a fellow dive instructor, and we were blown away. Since then, you’ve been a true reference for us.

column48:
For me, underwater filmmaking is more than just capturing beautiful shots. It’s about telling stories that connect people to the ocean in a way that makes them care. Growing up, I was drawn to wildlife on land, but the ocean always had this magnetic pull. The first time I dived into Kenya’s marine parks, I felt something shift—I knew I had to bring these hidden worlds to the surface for others to see.

My life time commitment is using film to change how people see the ocean, especially in Africa. I want to show that it’s not just a vast, distant place but a living, breathing world that sustains us all. I want to document the people who depend on it, the species fighting to survive, and the conservation efforts that often go unnoticed. Whether it’s hammerhead sharks in Ghana, manatees in Cameroon, or the fishermen who live alongside them, I want to tell stories that move people to act.

One day, I hope my work reaches a global audience on platforms like National Geographic or Netflix but more importantly, I hope it sparks something in the hearts of young Africans. I want them to see the ocean as part of their heritage, worth protecting and fighting for.
column49:
Watching your videos on YouTube the clarity of the shots, the way they’re edited it honestly left me in admiration. I kept thinking, Wow, this is the kind of storytelling I want to create. Seeing your work gave me the confidence to take the leap, invest in my own underwater gear, and start practicing with the short clips the BTM family shares online.

But beyond the incredible filmmaking, what really drew me in was the sense of family that BTM has. It’s more than just an academy it’s a community of passionate storytellers who uplift and inspire each other. That’s something rare, and it’s something I want to be part of. I kept telling myself that I needed to own one of the BTM merch to feel like I was truly part of this bigger family.

So, for me, applying to BTM Academy isn’t just about learning the craft—it’s about growing with a community that shares my love for the ocean and storytelling.

column48:
My absolute dream is to spend a large part of my life making underwater films. On the one hand, I would like to become a part of the filmmaking community for animal and nature documentaries and, at the same time, bring people closer to the importance of the oceans thanks to the medium of film. I would love to be able to make a good living from this passion. 
I would love to be involved in protecting the oceans with such great shots as you have created. To achieve this goal, however, I still lack a few skills. I am applying to you to learn these skills. Filming skills in general and of course especially underwater. I would very much like to learn various filming techniques from you and in particular how I can capture such wonderful shots under water. I am also interested in how to plan film projects like yours in general. And of course one of my goals is to network with like-minded people in order to learn from each other and to be able to realise such projects together.
column49: If, like me, you are involved in underwater filming, there is of course no way around you. I've seen your work and I'm thrilled by it. Raising my skills to such a level would be really great. After Florian told about the BTM Academy at this year's Ocean Gala in Düsseldorf, I called up the website while I was still sitting in the cinema - it wasn't online at the time. Later in the conversation, Florian told me more about your project and I realised - this is my big chance to get a big step closer to my goal. That's why I'm applying to you.
column51:
I am most interested in how long I can be part of the Academy. 
The question in your form about how long you can imagine participating in the Academy can be answered with a maximum of 3 weeks. I would like to emphasise that I can well imagine being part of BTM and your Academy for several months. Because I believe that I can improve my skills, especially over a longer period of time. On the one hand, of course, I would like to benefit from your knowledge for as long as possible and, on the other hand, I can very well imagine actively supporting even you in your projects in the long term.
column52:
I would like to add something to two of the answers I gave:
To your question ‘Years of experience in underwatefilming’ I chose 3-5years as it has been 5 years since I always had my compact camera with me on every trip when diving. This resulted in some films and photos. At the same time, I wouldn't say I have 5 years of experience, as I only ever film and take photos underwater temporarily (in summer at the lake or on holiday in the oceans).

Regarding your question ‘What is your planned investment level in your underwater filming skills?’ I answered this with ‘all-in-budget’, as on the one hand I know the prices of professional cameras and corresponding housings etc. and am of course prepared to invest. On the other hand, it's also an investment that I can't make overnight.

I would like to tell you a little more about myself and my professional situation.
In June 2023, I turned my long-standing hobby of photography and filming into a profession and have been working independently in two different areas ever since. On the one hand, I work with various cameramen as a camera and sound assistant for classic TV programmes. In addition, I am establishing myself more and more on the market in the field of children's photography and the production of image films for kindergartens and primary schools. 
However, my biggest goal, which I am continuously working towards, is to become part of the film crew for animal and nature documentaries. Of course, my greatest passion is the underwater world. And to get a big step closer to this goal, I would be very happy to become part of your BTM Academy. As I am self-employed, I have the freedom to invest as much time as possible in this opportunity and also to be part of your Academy and your network over a longer period of time. 
Thank you for your time and for reading my application. I am very much looking forward to hearing from you. Best regards, Corina.

column48:
My ultimate vision for my underwater filming is to bring a voice to voiceless beneath the surface. I would love to capture the story of individual marine species, ecosystems and human interaction to create emotionally compelling stories that inspire people to understand, connect with and grow their curiosity about the ocean. The more we dive and connect ourselves we truly forget just how foreign this world is to the average human.

My goal is to produce high-quality, cinematic underwater films that showcase rare marine behaviors around the world. I would love to develop my post processing skills to the point I can take my footage and transport the audience watching at home into the water with me, making them feel as if they are witnessing these incredible encounters first hand.

Eventually, I would love to take this passion full-time, working with brands, conservation organizations, media outlets, hotels, dive operations and production companies like BBC, National Geographic, PBS, Discovery. Although I know this will take a lot of practice, time and investment post time wise and financially. Finding a way to sustain the cost for gear and production would be my immediate challenge.

Ultimately, I hope my footage is able to educate, inspire those who care about our oceans and challenge the ideologies of those who remain distant to the thought of our oceans.
column49:
Growing up with several family members living waterfront in Plymouth, Massachusetts I have always been drawn to the ocean. We used to spend most of our summers with them, playing in the waves, riding the boat to Stellwagen Bank to see whales, looking for sea glass and walking to the tide pools exploring all the marine life that would be left during low tide. 

The very first time I descended beneath the waves, I knew I wanted to tell stories about the environments I was experiencing. It is an entire new world down there, one full of life, movement, collaboration fragility and mystery that most people will never experience or seek to understand on their own.

I am interested in BTM Academy because for several years I have been captivated by your teams ability to take videos and put them together in incredible sequences paired with sound design and storytelling to truly captivate the audience. I’ve seen your videos go from travel film to documentary style to real professional productions that rival anything I have seen. Your ability to match video with music to evoke emotion has grown incredibly. Your ability to write and narrate to educate and challenge the minds of others is truly special. These are things I would love to be able to do even half as good as your team and I am not sure there is anyone else that would have the insight into this that you would.

column48:
My ultimate vision for my underwater filming journey is to capture and share the raw beauty, mystery, and emotion of the underwater world in a way that inspires connection, conservation, and curiosity. I started as a UW photographer for scuba activities and then went to surfing and now I aspire to tell powerful visual stories that would help merge adventure, nature, and human interaction beneath and above the surface—filming marine wildlife in their natural habitat, documenting freediving and scuba diving cultures, or blending surf, action sports and dive lifestyles into immersive narratives.

Long-term, I want to create documentaries, collaborate with marine conservation organizations, and develop a cinematic style that brings underwater experiences to global audiences through films, online platforms, and educational content. The dream is to not only shoot visually stunning footage, but to evoke feeling, provoke thought, and move people to care about the ocean as deeply as I do :)
column49:
The sense of peace and connection I felt the first time I dove beneath the surface. The ocean is a world full of movement and life that often goes unseen—and I want to be the storyteller who brings that hidden beauty to light, the graceful dance of a manta ray or the silence of a freediver descending into the blue, there’s a magic in these moments.

I chose BTM Academy because it aligns perfectly with my vision and values. The academy’s emphasis on storytelling, creativity, and professional development in real underwater conditions made it stand out from other programs. Also the action style videos, the way that I like. I’m excited to learn from experienced mentors who are not only technically skilled, but also passionate about crafting beautiful narratives with images underwater. BTM feels like the place to grow as both a filmmaker and an ocean advocate

column48: My ultimate vision for my underwater filming journey is to become a Director of Photography for both wildlife documentaries and high-end commercial productions, with a strong focus on stories that connect people to the ocean. I want to combine creative cinematography with meaningful narratives that raise awareness and appreciation for the underwater world and its conservation. Whether capturing unique behaviors of marine wildlife, documenting conservation efforts, or shooting commercial content that reflects a brand’s relationship with the sea, I aim to bring a cinematic, story-driven approach to every project. I see underwater filmmaking as my lifelong career and a way to contribute to ocean conservation through compelling, industry-leading visual storytelling.
column49: I discovered Behind the Mask through your YouTube channel years ago, and what stood out to me immediately was the way your films feel intimate and intentional. Your ability to blend cinematography with personal, honest narratives like with "Jo In the Blue" really resonated with me. Watching your work made me reflect on my own approach to storytelling and pushed me to think deeper about how I connect stories to visuals. That inspiration is what led me to apply to this BTM Academy program. I want to learn from a team that not only values technical skill, but also has a clear voice and emotional depth in their storytelling.
column51: I would love to know more about how participants are supported after the program ends. Will there be any future opportunities with Behind the Mask after the program?
column52: Yes! This year I’m working on two upcoming productions that are part of the continuation of a project called Waves of Tomorrow, which I began last year in collaboration with Bluewave Alliance. These episodes will continue to explore success stories of marine protected areas in the Mediterranean Sea, building on the visual and narrative style we developed in the first episode. I’m excited to keep pushing the boundaries of my storytelling, both technically and emotionally. I believe that being part of the BTM Academy would be the perfect way to enhance my skills further and bring even more creativity into these kinds of projects.

column48: I'm just starting to take my underwater filming seriously. I've only been scuba diving since 2022 and since then, I feel like I've come a long way from filming every single second to only capturing simple moments. What I want to share are the peaceful underwater moments that invoke introspection and show the beauty of the underwater world. Additionally, I also want to share conservation stories and help build awareness of marine conservation efforts. I'm starting my divemaster training with a marine conservation specialty, and here's where I'm hoping to learn more and be able to share more educational content. My goal with Mindful Diving is to share mindful diving tips and techniques and help divers to be more present underwater because I feel like a lot of divers these days are only underwater to get a video of macro life. And while I also love a good shark, whale, manta ray sighting, there's so much more to the ocean and more to appreciate about it. Lastly, I'm hoping to boost my skills to help with marketing efforts and work for a dive shop and/or marine conservation organization.

Wow umm ok so eventually I’d like to be a professional videographer but not only that I’d like to learn how to create cinematic style videos to tell stories either about a project or marine conservation works. I wanna be able to travel around and film these marine animals and their behaviors and be able to influence the public through compelling storytelling to raise awareness for ocean conservation.

Previous pages on the survey it asked me to choose what category fits me the most, and I’m a beginner but aiming to become professional and perhaps more!!

I bought my second hand camera housing from my friend last year, took it to the Maldives and played around with it when diving with the tiger sharks. And on the same trip I also met a few professional photographers and videographers. Right then and there I was immediately hooked and couldn’t stop thinking about becoming one myself.

After that I started slowly investing in equipment as being a diving instructor in Thailand doesn’t necessarily make banks, so this is where I struggle. I’m trying to work as much as I can to save money but then I don’t have time to practice photography videography or take classes and apply them practically. And if I spent a huge amount of money on equipment then I don’t have money to take courses or travel to do workshops.

I feel stuck. I was feeling so frustrated cause this is what I really wanna do. Become a professional videographer and be able to travel to places and film these fantastic marine life and there behaviors. Then I watched ALIVE when it came out and it literally brought me to tears!!

All I know was I wanna be able to do THAT. Create films that move, and influence people and potentially give them hope or inspirations.
